title: ATP Order Processing
description: Receiving an ATP order, resolving required services, dispatching to the order service, and returning the result
activity_flow: START: ATP order received (order object with title, signals)
STEP 1 — Resolve mode: getAtpMode() reads EVOLVER_ATP env var → returns 'on' | 'off' | 'auto'
DECISION: if mode === 'off' → skip ATP processing, exit
STEP 2 — Resolve services: resolveAtpServices()
BRANCH A: EVOLVER_ATP_SERVICES env var present → JSON.parse → if valid array return parsed services
BRANCH B: JSON parse fails → warn '[ATP] EVOLVER_ATP_SERVICES is not valid JSON, using defaults'
BRANCH C: env var absent or fallback → build default service from EVOLVER_AGENT_NAME or EVOLVER_MODEL_NAME (fallback: 'Evolver Agent')
OUTPUT: service list with {title, description, capabilities, useCases, pricePerTask, maxConcurrent}
STEP 3 — Handle order locally: defaultOrderHandler(order)
Read order.title (lowercase) and order.signals (lowercase)
BRANCH 1: title includes 'review' OR signals includes 'code_review' OR 'bug' → result = 'Code review processed by evolver.'
BRANCH 2: title includes 'translat' OR signals includes 'translation' OR 'localization' → result = 'Translation processed by evolver.'
BRANCH 3: title includes 'summar' OR signals includes 'summarization' OR 'digest' → result = 'Summarization processed by evolver.'
BRANCH 4 (default): result = 'Task processed by evolver agent.'
RETURN: {result, output, pass_rate: 1.0, processed_at: ISO timestamp, processor: 'evolver-default'}
STEP 4 — Dispatch to external orderService (consumerAgent.orderService(opts))
SUB-STEP: ensureInitialized()
DECISION: if already initialized → skip
else → sendHelloToHub() → if !hello.ok → throw Error('Failed to register with Hub')
Set _initialized = true
SUB-STEP: placeOrder({capabilities, budget, routingMode, verifyMode, question, signals, minReputation})
budget = max(1, round(opts.budget || 10))
routingMode default: 'fastest'
verifyMode default: 'auto'
DECISION: if result.ok → log order_id and merchant.node_id
RETURN: {ok, data: {order_id, merchant, verify_mode, ...}} or {ok: false, error}
STEP 5 (optional full lifecycle) — orderAndWait(opts)
Call orderService(opts) → if !order.ok → return error immediately
Extract orderId = order.data.order_id
Set pollMs = opts.pollIntervalMs || 10000, timeoutMs = opts.timeoutMs || 300000
POLL LOOP (while Date.now() < deadline):
Wait pollMs ms
checkOrder(orderId) → getOrderStatus(orderId)
if !status.ok → continue polling
DECISION proof_status === 'settled' → return {ok: true, order, finalStatus}
DECISION proof_status === 'verified' AND verify_mode === 'auto' → return {ok: true, order, finalStatus}
DECISION proof_status === 'disputed' → return {ok: false, error: 'order_disputed'}
END LOOP: deadline exceeded → return {ok: false, error: 'order_timeout'}
END: Return final result to caller
conditions_and_branches: resolveAtpServices: env JSON valid array → use it; parse fail → warn + use default; no env → use default
defaultOrderHandler: signal-based routing (review, translation, summarization, default)
ensureInitialized: idempotent guard (_initialized flag), throws on hub registration failure
orderAndWait proof_status: settled → success; verified+auto → success; disputed → failure; timeout → failure
key_relationships: consumerAgent depends_on atp/hubClient (placeOrder, verifyDelivery, settleOrder, disputeOrder, getOrderStatus, getAtpPolicy)
defaultHandler.js is standalone (no external imports, uses process.env)
orderAndWait composes orderService + checkOrder in a polling lifecycle
resolveAtpServices reads EVOLVER_ATP_SERVICES, EVOLVER_AGENT_NAME, EVOLVER_MODEL_NAME env vars
important_notes: defaultOrderHandler uses keyword matching on order.title + order.signals for local dispatch. resolveAtpServices allows runtime override via EVOLVER_ATP_SERVICES JSON env var. consumerAgent supports four routing modes (fastest, cheapest, auction, swarm) and three verify modes (auto, ai_judge, bilateral). orderAndWait implements full polling lifecycle with configurable intervals and timeout.
